title: Unable to install buildah in linux fedora AWS EC2 instance
body:
Hi,
Iam trying to install buildah but throwing below errors.
dnf install buildah
yum install buildah   as well same errors for both

**_No match for argument: buildah
Error: Unable to find a match: buildah_**
I tried all the possibilities like downloading rpm packages and install but its not working. Can you pls help on this and install buildah in my linux fedora instance.

comments.body:
Which version of Fedora are you using?  Please provide more details.
If you are installing some custom Fedora, there is nothing we can do upstream.

It’s fedora 33

On Wed, 6 Sep 2023 at 1:22 PM, Valentin Rothberg ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Which version of Fedora are you using? Please provide more details.
> If you are installing some custom Fedora, there is nothing we can do
> upstream.
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5024#issuecomment-1707847308>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AJIXFEVVRWNS2JTEZ72FMH3XZATUDANCNFSM6AAAAAA4L6DPTU>
> .
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>
Fedora 33 is no longer supported, you need to update to F37 or 38.
Yes, please upgrade your systems.  Fedora 33 has hit [EOL 399 days ago](https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/releases/eol/).  That implies no security updates etc.

Hi,

Could you please suggest which Amazon linux version best to install buildah
through aws dashboard.

Thanks,
Vinay.

On Thu, 7 Sep 2023 at 1:20 PM, Valentin Rothberg ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Closed #5024 <https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5024> as

Sorry, I have no experience in or knowledge about Amazon Linux.  Please reach out to Amazon.
